artPrompt: A quick-fingered croupier hunched over a folding card table, three blank-backed cards mid-shuffle blurring beneath his darting hands, a sly sidelong grin half-lit by sickly neon. Scattered coins and the loom of a striped tent fill the smoky black background. The wide frame angles down on the rigged game, one hot-gold firelight raking his hands, embers adrift, rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: Three-Card Monte Dealer
text: When Three-Card Monte Dealer enters the battlefield, look at the top three cards of your library, shuffle them facedown, and 'let an opponent pick the lady.' Whichever they don't pick, you put into your hand; the rest go on top in any order.
flavorText: Keep your eye on the queen. The queen left the table an hour ago.
